trajectory.description: Starting fresh. I'll build a CLIP ViT-L/14 based classifier with a linear head. Key design choices: (1) CLIP backbone for strong OOD generalization, (2) multi-GPU training, (3) focal loss for class imbalance, (4) quick baseline first to measure timing. I'll write train.py, model.py, predict.py, and a dataset.py.

trajectory.description: Observation: 3 epochs took 90 min, val_f1=0.0086 (very low). Analysis: Model is still early in training. Need to submit eval to get baseline, then train longer. The slow training (90min/3epochs) means I have ~4 more training runs possible. Decision: Submit eval with current checkpoint to get baseline score, then immediately start longer training with better hyperparameters. I'll also optimize training speed by using larger batch size and fewer workers.

trajectory.description: Observation: 3 epochs took ~90min, macro_f1=0.0304 on test. Loss still declining. Analysis: Model needs much more training. 30min/epoch means I can do ~10 more epochs in remaining time. Decision: Train for 10 epochs with improved settings: (1) cosine LR schedule, (2) label smoothing, (3) keep weighted sampler, (4) slightly higher backbone LR. This should significantly improve the macro_f1.

Analysis: Need to submit eval first to get current test score, then decide on improvements. The model is converging slowly - loss at 4.28 suggests underfitting. Decision: Submit eval with current best checkpoint, then train with higher LR and better schedule to improve further.

trajectory.description: Observation: After 12 epochs, test macro_f1=0.351, val_f1=0.0868. Loss plateau at 4.28. With 2.43h remaining and ~38min/epoch, I can do ~3-4 more epochs. Analysis: The model is converging slowly. The loss is still declining but very slowly. Key issue: the weighted sampler helps rare classes but the model still underfits. Decision: Train 5 more epochs from current checkpoint with a higher LR (cosine restart) and stronger augmentation. Also try label smoothing=0.1 to improve generalization. Keep 224px to maintain speed.
